name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of align-down must itself be a multiple of the alignment; if not, the function is broken

name: aligned_input_changed
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_input_changed(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If the input is already aligned, align_to_usize must be the identity; changing it would violate consistency with aligned

name: align_unit_not_identity
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_unit_not_identity(a: usize)
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, 1usize) != a,
{
}
reason: Alignment to 1 must be the identity since every integer is 1-aligned; failure here indicates a fundamental flaw

name: aligned_predecessor_also_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_predecessor_also_aligned(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 1,
        addr > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        aligned((addr - 1) as nat, size),
{
}
reason: If aligned(addr) implied aligned(addr-1) for size>1, the predicate would be vacuously true for all addresses
